tfpt_expectation.statement: FLRW isotropy + at most a tiny mu4/PSL(2,C) orientation remnant; no mechanism for an intrinsic super-Hubble matter dipole => the number-count dipole excess MUST dissolve into clustering/mask/selection systematics (dissolution watchdog, same family as fixed-point-watchdog axes D-G)

observed.direction: aligned with CMB dipole within ~27 deg

claimed_legs.name: MALS/radio kinematic decomposition (Wagenveld+2025)
claimed_legs.reference: Wagenveld et al. 2025, A&A (arXiv:2504.xxxx; aa53397-24): joint kinematic + residual fit

claimed_legs.name: RMP colloquium status (Secrest, von Hausegger, Rameez, Mohayaee, Sarkar 2025)

clustering_mask_reassessments.name: FLASK reassessment S1 (no clustering dipole)
clustering_mask_reassessments.reference: arXiv:2511.00822 (ApJ 2026): lognormal LSS + shot noise + exact mask

clustering_mask_reassessments.name: octopole-marginalized reading (Abghari+2024)
clustering_mask_reassessments.reference: Abghari et al. 2024 (JCAP); contested by Secrest et al. 2025

today: every catalog sees D ~ 2x kinematic aligned with the CMB dipole; claimed significances 4.4-5.7 sigma (RMP 2025: '>5 sigma'), clustering/mask-aware reassessments 3.3-3.6 sigma. The error budget -- not the excess -- is the open question. TFPT requires the FLRW-violating reading to dissolve.
deciders: LSST + Euclid + SKA/MALS number-count dipoles (independent selections, deeper flux limits); DESI QSO spectroscopic dipole; joint clustering-marginalized analyses
kill_rule: a clustering-marginalized, mask-controlled NON-kinematic dipole at >= 5 sigma, replicated in >= 2 independent selections (e.g. LSST/Euclid + SKA-MALS), surviving the aligned-clustering scenario => strikes the FLRW foundation of the TFPT cosmology branch (flat budget, Lambda/H0 engine) -- not the compiler core
verdict_line: DIPOLE WATCHDOG: excess x2.1 real in every catalog; FLRW-violation significance contested (3.3-5.7 sigma) -> data_limited (watch). TFPT requires dissolution; a confirmed >=5 sigma intrinsic dipole would strike the FLRW-based cosmology branch.
